title: cBCI Matrix Multimodal Dataset
description: This multimodal dataset comprises simultaneous EEG and eye-tracking recordings from 21 healthy participants performing a matrix-based brain-computer interface spelling task. EEG data were acquired using a 24-channel dry electrode system at 300 Hz, while eye movements were recorded at 60 Hz using a portable eye tracker. The dataset includes calibration and copy-spelling task data with event markers for target and non-target stimulus presentations, providing a resource for investigating sensor fusion approaches in EEG-based BCI typing systems.
methods_description: EEG data were collected using a DSI-24 dry electrode cap (Wearable Sensing) at 300 Hz sampling rate with a bandwidth of 0.003–150 Hz from 13 electrode locations with linked-ear reference. Eye-tracking data were acquired using a Tobii Pro Nano eye tracker at 60 Hz. Participants performed a single-character-presentation matrix paradigm with 100 calibration inquiries (10 letters each at 4 Hz presentation rate). Stimuli were presented in white on black background with red fixation crosses. Timing fidelity was verified using the Matrix Time Test Task with photodiode validation (±10 ms tolerance).
